title: architecture_decisions
content: Our platform uses a microservices architecture with PostgreSQL for transactional data and BigQuery for analytics. We chose Kafka for event streaming between services. The authentication service uses JWT tokens with a 24-hour expiry. All API endpoints require rate limiting at 100 requests per minute per user.

title: deployment_guide
content: The application is deployed on Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with auto-scaling enabled. Each service runs in its own pod with resource limits of 512MB RAM and 0.5 CPU. We use Helm charts for deployment configuration. The CI/CD pipeline runs on GitHub Actions with automated testing and staging deployment before production.

title: data_pipeline_overview
content: Data flows from the PostgreSQL operational database through Kafka topics to BigQuery for analytics. We process approximately 10 million events per day. The ETL pipeline runs every 6 hours using Data Weaver with incremental loads based on the updated_at timestamp. Quality checks validate row counts and null percentages before loading to the analytics warehouse.
